reviews.author: Rob G
reviews.text: At a quick glance, the site appears somewhat unremarkable. However, upon deeper inspection, the stone work is superb and the cultural and historical significance of this site cannot be understated. The Citadel retains the symmetrical 4 gates which are still flanked by the earthen walls. This sprawling landmark is welm presereved, but could and should be further excavated to uncover more artifacts. In typical Vietnamese fashion... the abandoned fortress is being utilized in a practical way for contemporary needs...as farm fields. Overall, a wonderful place to just imagine the original purpose of the structure and take inthe energy and sense of pride the visitors have for this ancient fortress.

reviews.author: Mike Lynch
reviews.text: It was an enjoyable day. Quite a lot of walking so wear good footwear. Lots of exhibits to see spread over a wide area. For no good reason they don't allow you to leave through the entrance and insist on making you walk quite a distance, to get out. Which was the completely the opposite direction to where my bike was parked. Absolutely ridiculous given the heat and humidity. The attraction needs a lot of investment and general work to give it the wow factor...not sure people value it as an historic site or just a backdrop to take photos.

reviews.author: Oliver Parker
reviews.text: Worth visiting if you are passing through. There's a reasonable amount of information in Vietnamese and English on different boards around the site, although the museum is quite small. There is a ticket booth at the south gate but all the others are just open and you can roam about the site freely on foot or by motorbike. The entrance fee was 40k VND and the security guard was very helpful trying to explain where I could go and everything I could see, despite not speaking English. Overall it was interesting and worth a visit but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit (probably not worth a round trip out of tam coc / ninh binh city just for this unless you have time to spare).

reviews.author: antonio jones
reviews.text: Worth visiting if you are in the area.

reviews.author: Nicole Hien Pham
reviews.text: Should visit before 4pm if you travel by Bus and want to enter Sam Son Beach area. The bus no.2 only goes to Sam Son center by early line accordling to ticket seller of this bus line . If you are a history lover this is a good place to explore.

reviews.author: Ngo Hai Anh
reviews.text: Designated a World Heritage site in 2011, Ho Citadel still doesn't receive many foreign visitors. Built at the end of the 14th century, the inner walls and four main gates of the citadel are still standing. Called Thành Nhà Hồ in Vietnamese, the complex is surrounded by a fertile rural landscape. The walls are overgrown with creepers and grass, but still make an impressive sight rising from the rice fields. Vietnamese farmers go about their work, both outside and inside the citadel grounds; sitting down to rest in the shade of its arched gates during the hottest part of the day. The grand royal ruins and the agricultural work going on around it, remind me of sketches by Piranesi of Rome in the 18th century, when Italy was rediscovering its past, just as Vietnam is doing today. Located near the town of Vĩnh Lộc in Thanh Hóa Province, Ho Citadel can be reached by several rural back-roads. Although the enclosure is very large (close to 1km2) it's not immediately visible, especially if you arrive at either the north, east or west gates (the south gate being the main entrance). From some positions the wall is so overgrown it's almost indistinguishable from the farmland it abuts. The main entrance (10,000vnđ) is through the south gate, where there are guides available and a small museum. The latter tries very hard – with English language labels and information boards – but doesn't amount to much more that bits and pieces of pottery, cannonballs, bricks and tiles. The other gates also have small ticket offices, but most of the ticket inspectors seem to be either asleep or drunk and drowsy. If you avoid weekends and public holidays chances are you'll have the whole citadel to yourself, making you feel as though you're the first foreigner to have stumbled upon these imperial ruins.

reviews.author: Trung Lê Quang
reviews.text: My mother’s hometown is here. The citadel was built in the 15th century when Thăng Long Hanoi was attacked by Ming dynasty (old name of China) at that time. UNESCO had recognized this place as one of its heritage in Vietnam

reviews.author: Haggai Be'er
reviews.text: It's an intruding historical place. An old Royal Palace like the citadel in Hanoi but in a more raw condition. But really there isn't much left there to see, except the massive, 3 arched, old gate.

reviews.author: Kent Kuo
reviews.text: I can not believe that they are able to build the city wall by that big stone. And all brick with name on it.

reviews.author: Tram Nguyen
reviews.text: Definitely worth a visit. The historic site listed in UNESCO's world heritage has been constantly improved both in services for tourists and surrounding landscapes.

reviews.author: Staffan Nyman
reviews.text: It’s a nice place to bring the kids to at sunset or to fly kites. Information in both Vietnamese and English.

reviews.author: Brice Pissard
reviews.text: UNESCO site, it is free to visit, it can be visited in one hour, required to walk a bit to see the 4 doors.

reviews.author: Huy Pham
reviews.text: It is a great historical place to visit. Hoping in the next few years the local gov shall rebuild the wall and castle etc.

reviews.author: Maciej Kolodziejczyk
reviews.text: There's not much to see other than four monumental gates and long walls - all made from enormous stone blocks. There's a little museum with descriptions in English. I gave half a star for historical significance (World Heritage Site) and another half-star for the view of rice paddies from the citadel walls and friendly locals.

reviews.author: Kiên Phan
reviews.text: It’s an abandoned vestige but really enjoyed the locals here!

reviews.author: Bettina Allisat
reviews.text: Very beautiful citadel in the heart of Hanoi.

reviews.author: Tommy Long
reviews.text: Nothing to visit but the entrance fee is too much expensive

reviews.author: Mr Thang - Dusit Tran
reviews.text: Ho Citadel is the world heritage UNESCO in Thanh Hoa province

reviews.author: Daniel Lindestreng
reviews.text: Nice place but not so much to see. Appears to be an entry fee but we didn't pay.

reviews.author: Nghiem Dang
reviews.text: Great place to learn about Vietnam history

reviews.author: Leo Heidweiler
reviews.text: Impressive structure, and a very large area where you can drive around and explore stufe. The exhibition was not very big though.

reviews.author: Cao Viet Cuong
reviews.text: Nice culture, historic, architecture value place

reviews.author: AtKuDa Brat
reviews.text: nice and quiet.not much to see tho.no fees.can go through citadelle with bicycle/scooter

reviews.author: Lucas Ungaretti
reviews.text: WHS, 40k entry but quite boring. Small exhibit but not much too see besides the entrance gate. No one else there.

reviews.author: Stevie Avent
reviews.text: Lovely historical place to visit although there's very little structure remaining other than a scattering of stone walls and some of the large ceremonial portals into what was then the enclosed area. I guess over the centuries resources such as cut stones are far too valuable to be stacked around and doing nothing. There's also a dusty old museum which is quite interesting although English narratives are scarce.

reviews.author: Trọng Công Lê
reviews.text: Great citadel dated 600 years ago.

reviews.author: Mr. Khắc Thủy - MMO
reviews.text: This is the good site with its historical mean of Vietnam for visitor

reviews.author: Bruce Edwards
reviews.text: Amazing block work. The resources and ingenuity and the time frame it was built in, blew me away. Hot and dry inside. Ancient house on the western entry good.

reviews.author: Phillip Daw
reviews.text: There's not all that much old construction remaining but the sight is interesting. You see the local children playing in the walls and the villagers growing rice and corn within, just another day at work! You don't see other foreigners.

reviews.author: M. Véaigh
reviews.text: It cost 40,000 VND to get in, but there is nothing there! There is just a very old gatehouse with a road going through it that is under construction. Literally nothing else. This shouldn't cost any money, let alone 40K! Not worth checking out at all, avoid it!
